tools.name: docraptor_create_document
tools.description: Generate a PDF or Excel document from HTML (document_content) or a URL (document_url). Exactly one of document_content / document_url is required. By default the document is HOSTED and the tool returns a { download_url } you can fetch — ideal for agents (no large binary in the response). Set hosted:

tools.name: docraptor_get_document_status
tools.description: Check the status of an asynchronous document created with docraptor_create_document({async:true}). Returns status (queued | working | completed | failed), and when completed a download_url / download_id / number_of_pages. DocRaptor API: GET /status/{status_id}.

tools.name: docraptor_list_documents
tools.description: List metadata for documents created on this account, most recent first (paginated). Note: DocRaptor does not retain the rendered files themselves — this returns metadata such as name, type, test flag, and creation time. DocRaptor API: GET /docs.json.

tools.name: docraptor_list_ip_addresses
tools.description: List the IP addresses DocRaptor uses to fetch remote assets / document_url. Useful for allowlisting DocRaptor on a firewall or asset host. (DocRaptor notes these change over time — prefer HTTP Basic auth over IP allowlisting for securing assets.) DocRaptor API: GET /ips.json.

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: docraptor_create_document · Private data: docraptor_create_document, docraptor_get_document_status, docraptor_list_documents · Public sink: docraptor_create_document.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: docraptor_create_document · Private data: docraptor_create_document, docraptor_get_document_status, docraptor_list_documents · Public sink: docraptor_create_document

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
